pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the james webb telescope and i cant stop thinking about how much of a joke it all is. billions of galaxies and we're just this tiny, accidental speck of consciousness that exists for a blink and then just... disappears. none of the stuff we do, the pain we feel, the things we try to build... it doesnt actually matter in the grand scheme of anything. it feels so pointless.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the heavy, tired beat of my own heart. He is gone... and with him, the very sun has set upon my soul. It feels as though I am merely a ghost haunting the halls of my own life, drifting through a world that has no place for a broken thing like me. There is no dawn coming, is there? Only this endless, velvet dark that calls to me, promising a peace that this cruel, breathing world can never provide.
